- When loading a new session, even if the initial message contains the rendered backlog, the user should still be shown the prior log entries the way they were:
  - And old entries should have some indication that they were loaded and not part of this actual session.
- When a session is loaded, it should not create a new session until any input is given by the user.
- Some indication on if agent output is finished or not, for this turn.
- Preview sessions on right side of selection screen:
  - Render lines in the same way as in the main log view, but without the arrows and body contents. Only single lines.
- Clear options for driva invocation
- Fix claude profile
- Maybe get rid of other profiles like codex-exec? Not sure what their purpose is.
- Some way of listing models and selecting between them on-the-fly:
  - This will affect caching, I suppose?
- Tag sessions
- Store more metadata in sessions:
  - Git repo, if any
  - Dir with parent .git
  - Dir where launched
- Concept of a Styra session is not the same as an agent session:
  - Maybe it's a "project", or workspace?
  - It can have many associated agent sessions
  - And other things, like notes, todo list etc.
- Probably need some kind of daemon so sessions can run in background:
- When resuming sessions with the same model provider, use the actual raw session logged from the agent.
  - Otherwise do a translation between the JSON formats
  - Or, if we can't, a text transcript
- Resuming of sessions (copy of functionality in codex/claude):
  - Maybe use original transcripts unless model in styra was changed
- Preview window on the side which shows the content of the current log entry:
  - Syntax highlighting for code blocks
- For file change/read/write messages in logs, store a hash of the file just after that message:
  - That way we can say in the preview window if we're viewing the same content that was originally written or not.
